items.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
items.summary: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

items.title: OpenAI launches new initiative to help find and patch open source bugs
items.summary: OpenAI is using AI to help the open source community better protect itself.

items.title: Tesla pushes back on Autopilot narrative after fatal Texas crash
items.summary: Whether the Autopilot system was truly active, overridden, or malfunctioning likely won't be resolved until investigators finish combing through the vehicle's data logs.

items.title: Shareholders sue Uber’s board over sexual assaults, other incidents
items.summary: The lawsuit, led by a Detroit pension fund, alleges Uber's board and management has cut too many compliance corners, resulting in thousands of lawsuits.

items.title: The AI world is getting ‘loopy’
items.summary: The loop takes agentic AI a step further by authorizing a swarm of agents to work continuously in the background, endlessly.

items.title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
items.summary: Microsoft inked a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron, locking in decades of carbon emissions from a new natural gas power plant.

items.title: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal
items.summary: What does an AI company do after one of those not-acqui-hire deals? Groq raised money, is leaning into its neocloud business, and is hiring new execs.

items.title: Nvidia wants to cut data center water use, but that’s not the same as fixing AI’s water problem
items.summary: Nvidia announced a new cooling system that cuts water use inside the data center. But it does nothing to address AI's biggest water use — fossil fuel power plants.

items.title: Tata Electronics, a major tech supplier to Apple and Tesla, confirms data breach
items.summary: The incident comes as Tata Electronics expands its role in global technology supply chains.

items.title: A new unpatchable flaw in Apple chips opens the door to an iPhone jailbreak
items.summary: European offensive cybersecurity company Paradigm Shift released details of a flaw and a technique to exploit it that opens the door for hackers to unlock and break into older iPhones.

items.title: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal
items.summary: Google DeepMind and A24 are teaming up to build AI filmmaking tools.

items.title: Anthropic says Claude may want to see your ID
items.summary: Claude's chatbot may ask to verify your age and identity "in certain circumstances," such as with a passport or driver's license, according to a privacy policy change.

items.title: Amazon is testing Alexa+ in India with Hindi support
items.summary: Amazon is planning to increase the footprint of its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India and is inviting users in the country to test out a Hindi-language version.

items.title: SpaceX inks compute deal with Reflection AI, an open source AI lab
items.summary: Reflection AI will pay $150 million a month beginning July 1, 2026 through 2029 for immediate access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips and supporting hardware across SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis, Tennessee.

items.title: WhatsApp gets new chief as Meta taps India’s CRED founder Kunal Shah and invests $900M in startup
items.summary: WhatsApp gets a new boss, as Will Cathcart moves to a new role at Meta, while Shah steps down as CEO of Indian fintech giant CRED to replace Cathcart.

items.title: Instagram looks to take on streaming services with longer-form, episodic and live formats for its TV app
items.summary: Instagram is coming for streaming services like Netflix and Amazon Prime Video as it sets its ambitions for living room viewing.

items.title: The founder conference built for growth: TechCrunch Founder Summit pass rates increase June 26
items.summary: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 by June 26, 11:59 p.m. PT. Designed for founders first on November 4 in Boston. Register today.

items.title: Lucid Motors’ new CEO cuts 18% of staff to ‘simplify the company’
items.summary: The company is also eliminating a production shift at its Arizona factory to align "production plans with anticipated demand."

items.title: Seedcamp raises $320M for its new fund to expand its US footprint
items.summary: After 18 years of focusing on Europe, early-stage investor Seedcamp said that it has raised $320 million for its latest fund, which will see it expanding its presence in the United States.

items.title: Klue hack results in data breach at several cybersecurity firms
items.summary: Huntress, HackerOne, Jamf, Recorded Future, and Tanium are among the cybersecurity companies that had data stolen following an earlier breach at market research firm Klue.
